message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-10, latest commit). Confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; regression test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Be skeptical; find holes.

FINDING P10-MO-2:
from_records raised a raw AttributeError when a record's payload field is a non-mapping (list/str), violating the typed-error contract. Fix: ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__ raises ProvenanceError if payload is not a Mapping (before freeze_mapping calls .items()). VERIFY: from_records with a record whose payload=['x'] raises ProvenanceError (not AttributeError). Test: test_from_records_typed_error_on_non_mapping_payload. Confirm normal mapping payloads still construct.

Read the changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>; "python -m pytest"; gh for CI). Judge ONLY P10-MO-2. CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if a real residual gap; REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

a49b3f6 Merge pull request #170 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-9
edbb576 docs(hardening): record pass 9 — actual-CI-execution + fix-regression each caught a HIGH
22cbcad fix(hardening-p9): wire ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS into the capture hook so [REDACTED] actually scrubs (P9-PII-1 concern)
harden/pass-10

1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	import threading
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, cast
39	
40	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	
43	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
44	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
45	
46	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
47	
48	
49	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
50	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
51	
52	
53	def _contains_set(value: Any) -> bool:
54	    """True if a frozen payload holds a (frozen)set leaf anywhere (#P10-MO-1)."""
55	    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
56	        return True
57	    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
58	        return any(_contains_set(v) for v in value.values())
59	    if isinstance(value, (tuple, list)):
60	        return any(_contains_set(v) for v in value)
61	    return False
62	
63	
64	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
65	class ProvenanceEntry:
66	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
67	
68	    seq: int
69	    kind: str
70	    summary: str
71	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
72	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
73	    prev_hash: str
74	    entry_hash: str
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
77	        # #P10-MO-2: a non-mapping payload (a list/str slipped into a trail record) must surface as
78	        # the module's typed ProvenanceError, not a raw AttributeError from freeze_mapping calling
79	        # .items() on it — a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail relies on it.
80	        if not isinstance(self.payload, Mapping):
81	            raise ProvenanceError(
82	                f"provenance payload must be a mapping; got {type(self.payload).__name__}"
83	            )
84	        frozen = freeze_mapping(self.payload)
85	        # #P10-MO-1: a set/frozenset payload value is hashable (freeze accepts it) but does NOT
86	        # round-trip through to_records→from_records — unfreeze turns it into a sorted list, which
87	        # re-freezes to a tuple, so the recomputed digest differs and verify_records SILENTLY fails
88	        # on a legitimately-recorded entry. Reject it loudly at record time instead; callers should
89	        # pass a sorted list/tuple (which round-trips and digests identically).
90	        if _contains_set(frozen):
91	            raise ProvenanceError(
92	                "provenance payload cannot contain a set/frozenset value (it does not survive the "
93	                "to_records/from_records round-trip and would break verification); use a sorted "
94	                "list or tuple instead"
95	            )
96	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", frozen)
97	
98	
99	def _digest(
100	    seq: int,
101	    kind: str,
102	    summary: str,
103	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
104	    timestamp: str,
105	    prev_hash: str,
106	    secret: bytes | None,
107	) -> str:
108	    try:
109	        msg = canonical_json(
110	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
111	        ).encode("utf-8")
112	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
113	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
114	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
115	        raise ProvenanceError(
116	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
117	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
118	        ) from None
119	    if secret is None:
120	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
121	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
122	
123	
124	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
125	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
126	
127	
128	class ProvenanceTrail:
129	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
130	
131	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
132	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
133	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
134	    """
135	
136	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
137	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
138	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
139	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
140	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
141	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
142	        # serialize the read-compute-append so concurrent record() calls cannot interleave and
143	        # corrupt the seq/prev_hash chain (#114)
144	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
145	
146	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
147	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail. Thread-safe."""
148	        if not kind:
149	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
150	        with self._lock:
151	            return self._record_locked(kind, summary, payload)
152	
153	    def _record_locked(
154	        self, kind: str, summary: str, payload: Mapping[str, Any]
155	    ) -> ProvenanceEntry:
156	        seq = len(self._entries)
157	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
158	        try:
159	            instant = self._clock()
160	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
161	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
162	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
163	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
164	        try:
165	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
166	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
167	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
168	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
169	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
170	        except ProvenanceError:
171	            raise
172	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
173	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
174	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
175	        try:
176	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
177	                seq=seq,
178	                kind=kind,
179	                summary=summary,
180	                payload=payload,
181	                timestamp=timestamp,
182	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
183	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
184	            )
185	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
186	            raise ProvenanceError(
187	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
188	            ) from None
189	        self._entries.append(entry)
190	        return entry
191	
192	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
193	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
194	
195	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
196	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
197	        """
198	
199	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
200	            try:
201	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
202	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
203	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
204	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
205	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
206	                raise ProvenanceError(
207	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
208	                ) from None
209	            self.record(
210	                "gate",
211	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
212	                gate=gate,
213	                approved=approved,
214	                reason=reason,
215	                evidence=evidence,
216	            )
217	
218	        return _record
219	
220	    @property
221	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
222	        return tuple(self._entries)
223	
224	    @property
225	    def head(self) -> str:
226	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
227	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
228	
229	    def __len__(self) -> int:
230	        return len(self._entries)
231	
232	    def verify(self) -> None:
233	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
234	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
235	
236	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
237	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
238	        return entries_to_records(self._entries)
239	
240	
241	def entries_to_records(
242	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
243	) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
244	    """Serialize loose entries (e.g. ``StudyResult.provenance``) to plain dicts (#F-045).
245	
246	    The same shape :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` produces, so a caller holding only the
247	    returned entries — not the trail object — can still persist a re-checkable trail.
248	
249	    Keying is NOT embedded (pass 5, #H-017): a keyed (HMAC) trail and an unkeyed (SHA-256) trail
250	    serialize identically — the records carry no key material and no trusted algorithm marker (one
251	    would be spoofable since it is not itself part of the per-entry digest). The verifier must
252	    therefore know out-of-band whether the trail was keyed and supply the same ``secret`` to
253	    :func:`from_records` / :func:`verify_records`; that is deliberate key management, not a gap —
254	    ``verify_records`` fails closed under the wrong/absent secret.
255	    """
256	    return tuple(
257	        {
258	            "seq": e.seq,
259	            "kind": e.kind,
260	            "summary": e.summary,
261	            # deep-thaw nested FrozenDict/tuple values so the record is plain + JSON-serializable
262	            # (#F-045): dict(e.payload) only shallow-converts and leaves nested FrozenDicts (e.g.
263	            # the baseline 'determinism' map) which json.dumps cannot encode.
264	            "payload": cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(e.payload)),
265	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
266	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
267	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
268	        }
269	        for e in entries
270	    )
271	
272	
273	def verify_records(
274	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
275	    *,
276	    secret: bytes | None = None,
277	) -> None:
278	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
279	
280	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
281	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
282	    """
283	    prev = _GENESIS
284	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
285	        if e.seq != i:
286	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
287	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
288	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
289	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
290	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
291	            raise ProvenanceError(
292	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
293	            )
294	        prev = e.entry_hash
295	
296	
297	def from_records(
298	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
299	    *,
300	    secret: bytes | None = None,
301	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
302	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
303	    _required = ("seq", "kind", "summary", "payload", "timestamp", "prev_hash", "entry_hash")
304	    rebuilt: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
305	    for i, r in enumerate(records):
306	        missing = [k for k in _required if k not in r]
307	        if missing:
308	            # A malformed/incompatible trail record must surface as the module's typed error, not a
309	            # raw KeyError, so a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail file
310	            # covers this case too (pass 4, #G-012).
311	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance record {i} is missing field(s) {missing}")
312	        rebuilt.append(
313	            ProvenanceEntry(
314	                seq=r["seq"],
315	                kind=r["kind"],
316	                summary=r["summary"],
317	                payload=r["payload"],
318	                timestamp=r["timestamp"],
319	                prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
320	                entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
321	            )
322	        )
323	    entries = tuple(rebuilt)
324	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
325	    return entries
326

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git show --stat HEAD | head -40 && echo "---DIFF provenance---" && git show HEAD -- src/assay_engine/provenance.py
input.description: Show diff for provenance in latest commit

content.content:
commit d890ed3cd1afc6f960ba9bfa434c16842d122fae
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 13:34:33 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
    
    All 2-agent verified TRUE_POSITIVE. Two are fix-regressions of pass-9's own fixes.
    
    - SEC-10-1 (high): libpq PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR/PGSERVICE env vars override/supply the host even
      when the DSN looks loopback (PGHOSTADDR wins over host=localhost) → graph state off-box
      despite require_local_uri passing. Fix: _assert_local_libpq_env() validates PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR
      (loopback/socket, comma-split) and rejects PGSERVICE/PGSERVICEFILE (a service file can
      redirect off-box, unvalidatable inline), called from get_postgres_connection_string.
    - P10-PII-1 (high): the operator's real LAN topology (gateway [REDACTED], host
      [REDACTED], interface) leaked into committed transcripts from captured route/lsof
      output — no IP rule. Fix: RFC1918 redaction (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16) with digit/dot
      lookarounds (catches the \n-glued form, never matches inside a longer number); loopback
      127.x, 0.0.0.0 and public IPs preserved. Re-scrubbed corpus (literal private IPs now 0).
    - P10-CI-1 (high, fix-reg of CI-1): require_green_ci.sh checked only the latest run's
      conclusion, not its headSha → a stale green run certified a newer untested HEAD. Fix:
      fetch headSha and require it == git rev-parse origin/<branch>; fail closed if no completed
      run for the current commit.
    - P10-MO-1 (high): a set/frozenset payload is hashable but does NOT round-trip
      (unfreeze→list→re-freeze→tuple) so verify_records SILENTLY failed on a rebuilt chain. Fix:
      ProvenanceEntry rejects set/frozenset payload leaves loudly at record time (use a sorted
      list/tuple, which round-trips and digests identically).
    - P10-MO-2 (med): from_records raised a raw AttributeError on a non-mapping payload field.
      Fix: ProvenanceEntry raises typed ProvenanceError when payload is not a Mapping.
    - P10-PII-2 (low, fix-reg of P9-PII-1): the machine-hostname rule lacked a leading \b →
      substring over-redaction (host 'host' redacted inside 'localhost'). Fix: leading \b.
    - REL-1 (low, fix-reg of pass-8 K-REL-1): SIGTERM handler swallowed the signal when
      getsignal() returns None (prior handler installed from C). Fix: treat None like SIG_DFL and
      re-deliver the default terminate.
    
    Regression tests for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean; 575 passed,
    5 skipped; coverage floor enforced.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

 scripts/capture_transcripts.py                     |  18 +-
---DIFF provenance---
commit d890ed3cd1afc6f960ba9bfa434c16842d122fae
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 13:34:33 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p10): 7 confirmed incl. 4 HIGH (2 security, 1 privacy, 1 provenance)
    
    All 2-agent verified TRUE_POSITIVE. Two are fix-regressions of pass-9's own fixes.
    
    - SEC-10-1 (high): libpq PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR/PGSERVICE env vars override/supply the host even
      when the DSN looks loopback (PGHOSTADDR wins over host=localhost) → graph state off-box
      despite require_local_uri passing. Fix: _assert_local_libpq_env() validates PGHOST/PGHOSTADDR
      (loopback/socket, comma-split) and rejects PGSERVICE/PGSERVICEFILE (a service file can
      redirect off-box, unvalidatable inline), called from get_postgres_connection_string.
    - P10-PII-1 (high): the operator's real LAN topology (gateway [REDACTED], host
      [REDACTED], interface) leaked into committed transcripts from captured route/lsof
      output — no IP rule. Fix: RFC1918 redaction (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16) with digit/dot
      lookarounds (catches the \n-glued form, never matches inside a longer number); loopback
      127.x, 0.0.0.0 and public IPs preserved. Re-scrubbed corpus (literal private IPs now 0).
    - P10-CI-1 (high, fix-reg of CI-1): require_green_ci.sh checked only the latest run's
      conclusion, not its headSha → a stale green run certified a newer untested HEAD. Fix:
      fetch headSha and require it == git rev-parse origin/<branch>; fail closed if no completed
      run for the current commit.
    - P10-MO-1 (high): a set/frozenset payload is hashable but does NOT round-trip
      (unfreeze→list→re-freeze→tuple) so verify_records SILENTLY failed on a rebuilt chain. Fix:
      ProvenanceEntry rejects set/frozenset payload leaves loudly at record time (use a sorted
      list/tuple, which round-trips and digests identically).
    - P10-MO-2 (med): from_records raised a raw AttributeError on a non-mapping payload field.
      Fix: ProvenanceEntry raises typed ProvenanceError when payload is not a Mapping.
    - P10-PII-2 (low, fix-reg of P9-PII-1): the machine-hostname rule lacked a leading \b →
      substring over-redaction (host 'host' redacted inside 'localhost'). Fix: leading \b.
    - REL-1 (low, fix-reg of pass-8 K-REL-1): SIGTERM handler swallowed the signal when
      getsignal() returns None (prior handler installed from C). Fix: treat None like SIG_DFL and
      re-deliver the default terminate.
    
    Regression tests for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean; 575 passed,
    5 skipped; coverage floor enforced.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/provenance.py b/src/assay_engine/provenance.py
index 39c6c75..0ed7ee1 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/provenance.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/provenance.py
@@ -50,6 +50,17 @@ class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
     """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
 
 
+def _contains_set(value: Any) -> bool:
+    """True if a frozen payload holds a (frozen)set leaf anywhere (#P10-MO-1)."""
+    if isinstance(value, (set, frozenset)):
+        return True
+    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
+        return any(_contains_set(v) for v in value.values())
+    if isinstance(value, (tuple, list)):
+        return any(_contains_set(v) for v in value)
+    return False
+
+
 @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
 class ProvenanceEntry:
     """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
@@ -63,7 +74,26 @@ class ProvenanceEntry:
     entry_hash: str
 
     def __post_init__(self) -> None:
-        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
+        # #P10-MO-2: a non-mapping payload (a list/str slipped into a trail record) must surface as
+        # the module's typed ProvenanceError, not a raw AttributeError from freeze_mapping calling
+        # .items() on it — a caller catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail relies on it.
+        if not isinstance(self.payload, Mapping):
+            raise ProvenanceError(
+                f"provenance payload must be a mapping; got {type(self.payload).__name__}"
+            )
+        frozen = freeze_mapping(self.payload)
+        # #P10-MO-1: a set/frozenset payload value is hashable (freeze accepts it) but does NOT
+        # round-trip through to_records→from_records — unfreeze turns it into a sorted list, which
+        # re-freezes to a tuple, so the recomputed digest differs and verify_records SILENTLY fails
+        # on a legitimately-recorded entry. Reject it loudly at record time instead; callers should
+        # pass a sorted list/tuple (which round-trips and digests identically).
+        if _contains_set(frozen):
+            raise ProvenanceError(
+                "provenance payload cannot contain a set/frozenset value (it does not survive the "
+                "to_records/from_records round-trip and would break verification); use a sorted "
+                "list or tuple instead"
+            )
+        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", frozen)
 
 
 def _digest(

Now let me examine a critical detail: `from_records` catches `(ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError)` in `_record_locked`, but `from_records` itself calls `ProvenanceEntry(...)` directly without a try/except. Let me verify the test and that the `ProvenanceError` propagates correctly from `from_records`.

content.text:
Now let me verify the test discriminates (fails if reverted) and run the actual tests. Let me check the discrimination by examining whether the `["not","a","mapping"]` payload would reach `__post_init__`.

The key risk: does `from_records` propagate `ProvenanceError` from `ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__`? `from_records` calls `ProvenanceEntry(...)` directly with no try/except, so `ProvenanceError` propagates naturally. Let me run the test and verify discrimination.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python - <<'EOF'
# Discrimination: simulate the reverted state (no isinstance guard) on a non-mapping payload.
from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
try:
    freeze_mapping(["not","a","mapping"])
    print("REVERT: no error -> NON-DISCRIMINATING")
except Exception as e:
    print("REVERT raises:", type(e).__name__, "-", e)

# Now confirm what _digest would do BEFORE __post_init__ in from_records path:
# from_records builds ProvenanceEntry directly; the corrupt record payload=list reaches __post_init__.
from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceError, from_records, ProvenanceTrail
t = ProvenanceTrail(); t.record("k","s",x=1)
recs=[dict(r) for r in t.to_records()]
recs[0]["payload"]=["not","a","mapping"]
try:
    from_records(recs)
    print("FIXED: no error??")
except ProvenanceError as e:
    print("FIXED raises ProvenanceError:", e)
except Exception as e:
    print("FIXED raises OTHER:", type(e).__name__, e)

# str payload too
recs2=[dict(r) for r in t.to_records()]; recs2[0]["payload"]="astring"
try:
    from_records(recs2)
except ProvenanceError as e:
    print("str payload -> ProvenanceError OK")
except Exception as e:
    print("str payload -> OTHER", type(e).__name__)

# normal mapping still constructs
print("normal len:", len(from_records(t.to_records())))
EOF

content.content:
REVERT raises: AttributeError - 'list' object has no attribute 'items'
FIXED raises ProvenanceError: provenance payload must be a mapping; got list
str payload -> ProvenanceError OK
normal len: 1

content.text:
Confirmed. The revert produces a raw `AttributeError` (test would fail since it expects `ProvenanceError`), and the fix produces `ProvenanceError`. The test discriminates.

One subtlety to check: `verify_records` runs `_digest` over `dict(payload)` — but since `__post_init__` raises before the entry is appended/verified, the typed error fires first. Also note `from_records` doesn't wrap `ProvenanceEntry(...)` in try/except, so if some other `TypeError` arose it'd leak — but for this finding's scope (non-mapping payload), the guard handles it cleanly. Let me confirm the full provenance suite and gates pass.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q tests/test_provenance.py 2>&1 | tail -8

content.text:
All 18 provenance tests pass. Let me check the str-payload edge: a string is iterable but not a `Mapping`, correctly caught. The fix is at the right layer (the dataclass invariant in `__post_init__`), covers all `from_records`/`record` entry points uniformly, and the guard precedes `freeze_mapping`.

Assessment complete.

input.rationale: P10-MO-2 fix is appropriate and complete. ROOT CAUSE & LAYER: The raw AttributeError originated from freeze_mapping calling .items() on a non-Mapping payload. The fix adds an isinstance(self.payload, Mapping) guard in ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__ (src/assay_engine/provenance.py:80-83), BEFORE freeze_mapping is invoked. This is the correct layer — the dataclass invariant — so every construction path (from_records, record/_record_locked) is covered uniformly, not just from_records. APPROPRIATE: It raises the module's typed ProvenanceError with a clear message, restoring the typed-error contract that callers catching ProvenanceError to handle a corrupt trail rely on. from_records calls ProvenanceEntry(...) directly with no swallowing try/except, so the ProvenanceError propagates cleanly (verified empirically). COMPLETE/DISCRIMINATING: I verified the regression test tests/test_provenance.py::test_from_records_typed_error_on_non_mapping_payload (line 229) — it corrupts recs[0]['payload']=['not','a','mapping'] and asserts pytest.raises(ProvenanceError). I confirmed the revert behavior: freeze_mapping(['x']) raises AttributeError ('list' object has no attribute 'items') — so with the guard reverted, from_records raises AttributeError, the test's pytest.raises(ProvenanceError) would FAIL. The test therefore discriminates. I also independently confirmed str payloads ('astring') are caught (str is iterable but not a Mapping), and that normal Mapping payloads still construct and verify (from_records(t.to_records()) returns 1 entry; full provenance suite 18 passed). The Mapping check uses typing.Mapping (runtime-checkable ABC), correctly accepting dict and FrozenDict while rejecting list/str/tuple.

content.text: Review complete. P10-MO-2 is CONFIRMED — the fix is both appropriate and complete, and the regression test discriminates (fails if reverted).
